zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Cogan於1164年出生在威爾士格拉莫爾根可甘時，他的父親William De Cogan46歲，母親Margaret Windsor40歲。他於1174年與Basilie de Riddlesford在英國威爾斯格拉莫爾根可甘結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1238年在愛爾蘭庫迪 科克金薩爾享年74歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard de Cogan于1164年出生在威尔士格拉莫爾根可甘时，他的父亲William De Cogan46岁，母亲Margaret Windsor40岁。他于1174年与Basilie de Riddlesford在英国威尔斯格拉莫爾根可甘结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1238年在爱尔兰庫迪 科克金薩爾享年74岁。

nameMeanings.name: Cogan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of Jewish (from Ukraine) Kogan, from the Russian pronunciation of Cohen .

Irish and Welsh (of Norman origin): habitational name from Cogan near Cardiff, which may have been named with a Welsh word meaning ‘bowl, depression’. In 1166 Milo de Cogan, held two fees near Penarth (Glamorganshire) of William earl of Gloucester. Members of this Norman family probably account for the name's appearance in 13th-century Berwickshire. By this time the family was also present in Ireland, associated with counties Limerick and Cork. It may have been the Irish branch that later brought the name to southwest Scotland.

variants.name: Cogen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Dorith.  German Erwin.

variants.name: Cohen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Meyer, Isadore, Emanuel, Moshe, Shlomo, Myer, Avi, Yehuda, Chaim.
Jewish: from Hebrew kohen ‘priest’. Priests are traditionally regarded as members of a hereditary caste descended from Aaron, brother of Moses. See also Kaplan .
Irish (Galway and Sligo): variant of   Coyne  .

variants.name: Coggins
meanings: English: variant of Cogan , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Coggan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Brigid, Conal, Seamus.  Jewish Hyman.
Americanized form of Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic) Kogan, from the Russian pronunciation of Cohen .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

historicEvents.description: Arrival of Normans at Baginbun in Co. Wexford thus started 800 year struggle between English and Irish.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Cogan was born in 1164, in Cogan, Glamorgan, Wales, his father, William De Cogan, was 46 and his mother, Margaret Windsor, was 40. He married Basilie de Riddlesford in 1174, in Cogan, Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1238, in Kinsale, County Cork, Ireland, at the age of 74.

fr.Cogan, Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom: Cogan, Glamorgan, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Cogan est né en 1164, à Cogan, Glamorgan, Galles, son père, William De Cogan, avait 46 ans et sa mère, Margaret Windsor, avait 40 ans. Il a épousé Basilie de Riddlesford en 1174, à Cogan, Glamorgan, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1238, à Kinsale, County Cork, Irlande, à l’âge de 74 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard de Cogan 1164 in Cogan, Glamorgan, Wales geboren wurde, war sein Vater William De Cogan 46 und seine Mutter Margaret Windsor 40. Er hat 1174 in Cogan, Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom Basilie de Riddlesford geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1238 im Alter von 74 Jahren in Kinsale, County Cork, Irland.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard de Coganが1164年にウェールズグラモーガンコガンで生まれた時，父William De Coganは46歳，母Margaret Windsorは40歳であった。1174年にイギリスウェールズグラモーガンコガンでBasilie de Riddlesfordと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1238年にアイルランドコウントイ コルクキンサルで74歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Cogan(이)가 1164년 웨일스 글램르건 코건에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William De Cogan(은)는 46세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Windsor(은)는 40세였다. 그는 1174년 영국 웨일스 글램르건 코건에서 Basilie de Riddlesford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1238년 아일랜드 카운티 코르크 킨세일에서 74세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Cogan родился в 1164 г. в местности коган, глэморган, Уэльс, его отцу по имени William De Cogan было 46 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Windsor, 40. Он женился на женщине по имени Basilie de Riddlesford в 1174 г. в местности коган, глэморган, Уэльс, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1238 г. в местности кинсеил, коунти корк, Ирландия в возрасте 74 лет.

Murió en 1238, en Kinsale, County Cork, Irlanda, a la edad de 74 años.
